_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identify components or elements, allocate security functions to those elements, and describe the relationships between the elements.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data classification, sensitivity labeling, and the architectural controls required to enforce data access, privacy, and residency requirements by design.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.444), DQ-K-014 (DQ, cosine: 0.435).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.1/10 (raw 0.512). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [allocate, components, describe, elements, functions, identify] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, architectural, classification, controls, data, design] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.857 (P=0.856, R=0.859). Balanced P/R — the 17-word DCWF element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.1%). "Identify components or elements, allocate security functions to those elements," and "Knowledge of data classification, sensitivity labeling, and the architectural co" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.463 ≈ STS 0.512 (gap 0.049). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [none] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [allocate, components, describe, elements, functions, identify]. WIDAI terms: [access, architectural, classification, controls, data, design].
